problems.title: Matrix Rotation
problems.statement: Given an n×n matrix, rotate it 90 degrees clockwise and print the result, one row per line, space-separated.
problems.starterCode:
n = int(input())
grid = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(n)]
# rotate 90 degrees clockwise and print each row

problems.title: Circular Shift
problems.statement: Given a string and a shift amount k, right-shift the string circularly by k positions (the last k characters move to the front). If k is larger than the string's length, use k % len(s).
problems.starterCode:
s = input()
k = int(input())
# right circular shift by k and print the result

problems.title: Largest Connected Island
problems.statement: Given a grid of 0's and 1's, find the size of the largest group of 1's that are connected up/down/left/right (an "island").
constraints: First line: rows and cols. Next `rows` lines: `cols` characters each, '0' or '1'.
problems.starterCode:
rows, cols = map(int, input().split())
grid = [input() for _ in range(rows)]
# flood-fill each unvisited '1' and track the largest island size

problems.title: Exponent Race
problems.statement: Compute ab mod m. A naive loop multiplying b times is O(b); fast (binary) exponentiation — squaring and halving the exponent each step — does it in O(log b). Try implementing fast exponentiation yourself rather than using Python's built-in pow(a, b, m), to get the practice.
constraints: 0 ≤ b can be large — a slow O(b) loop may time out on the largest test.
problems.starterCode:
a, b, m = map(int, input().split())
# compute (a ** b) % m efficiently and print it
